\documentclass{article}
\begin{document}
\section{Production network}
Assume $\rho(\Omega)<1$ throughout the document.

The model has $J$ industries.

Industries are indexed by $i$ and $j$.

The matrix uses the user-by-supplier convention.

The notation below defines the Leontief object.

Let $\Omega\in\mathbb{R}^{J\times J}_{+}$.
\begin{equation}
  \mathcal L=(I-\Omega)^{-1}=I+\Omega+\Omega^2+\cdots
  \label{eq:leontief}
\end{equation}
Here $I$ is the $J\times J$ identity matrix and powers collect upstream paths.
\end{document}
